name: Fighter: Interception Fighting Style Reaction
description: A level 3 Fighter (Bram, fighting style: interception, longsword equipped) stands adjacent to a Wizard ally (Elara). A Hobgoblin attacks Elara across two rounds. Round 1: server raises an 'interception' opportunity on Bram, player accepts, server reduces damage by 1d10 + Bram's proficiency bonus (1d10=3 + 2 = 5; 8 - 5 = 3 final). Round 2: a higher 1d10 reduction (10 + 2 = 12) drops damage to 0 -- Elara's HP is unchanged AND no concentration save event is emitted (zero-damage suppression). Asserts exact HP after each round. BUG-FS-1 is RESOLVED -- the hasInterceptionStyle / hasWeaponEquipped flags are now propagated from buildCombatResources() into combatant.resources via initiative-handler.ts::assembleCombatantResources().

monsters.comment: Hobgoblin 5 ft from Elara, 5 ft diagonal from Bram so Bram is within Interception's ally-scan reach.
monsters.name: Hobgoblin Soldier

input.text: I intercept any blow aimed at Elara!

actions.comment: High initiative so PCs go first (Bram 21).
Force the Hobgoblin to target Elara so Bram is the ally-scan protector (adjacent to Elara).

Round 1 server-side dice queue: [18, 5, 3]. Slot 1 (18): attack d20 -> 18+5=23 vs AC 12 -> HIT. Slot 2 (5): damage 1d8 -> 5+3=8 raw damage. Slot 3 (3): Interception 1d10 -> reduction 3 + profBonus(2) = 5; final damage = max(0, 8-5) = 3. Elara: 20 -> 17.

Server raises an 'interception' reaction opportunity on Bram. Proves BUG-FS-1 is fixed -- before the fix, no reaction was offered because hasInterceptionStyle / hasWeaponEquipped were not propagated into combatant.resources.
Accept Interception. Server rolls 1d10 (=3) + profBonus(2) = 5 reduction; 8 - 5 = 3 final damage applied to Elara. Marks Bram.resources.reactionUsed=true. Emits InterceptionApplied event.
Round 1 result: Elara HP = 20 - 3 = 17 (damage was reduced from 8 to 3 by Interception).
Bram untouched (Hobgoblin targeted Elara). Confirms damage did not spill over.
Round 2 begins. Bram's turn -> his reactionUsed auto-resets so Interception is available again.
Round 2 monster decision: Hobgoblin attacks Elara again with Longsword.

Round 2 server-side dice queue: [18, 5, 10]. Slot 1 (18): attack d20 -> hit. Slot 2 (5): damage 1d8 -> 5+3=8 raw damage. Slot 3 (10): Interception 1d10 -> reduction 10 + profBonus(2) = 12; final damage = max(0, 8-12) = 0. Elara HP unchanged at 17. Because final damage is 0, NO concentration-save event is emitted (suppressed at zero damage).
Accept Interception again. Reduction (12) >= raw damage (8) -> final damage = 0. InterceptionApplied event still emitted, but no damage applied.
Round 2 result: Elara HP still 17 -- the full hit was completely absorbed by Interception.
Bram still untouched after two rounds.
Reach round 3 Bram turn -- proves the attack-reaction pipeline cleanly completed twice.
